title: viola lanceolata subsp. vittata
intro: Viola lanceolata subsp. vittata, also known as the fragrant pansy or heart's delight, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the violet family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Viola lanceolata subsp. vittata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has also been used in traditional medicine to make salves and creams for topical application.
